activity: Clarify Scrum concepts and roles (e.g., understanding Sprint events, accountabilities, or artifacts.)
Explore practices and techniques (e.g., estimation methods, refinement approaches, facilitation patterns.)
Get practical resources and examples (e.g., recommended tools, sample templates, or checklists to implement practices.)
Simulate conversations to create learning or practice scenarios for others – e.g., generating realistic role-play cases or Q&A dialogues to use in training sessions or workshops you lead.
Simulate conversations to coach or mentor team members – e.g., helping a developer or Product Owner adopt Scrum values, improve backlog management, or grow in their role.
Define candidate non-functional requirements
Write candidate Product Backlog items (e.g., from requirements document or product vision)
Define how to split Product Backlog items to fit a Sprint
Evaluate the quality of a Product Backlog item description
Improve the description of a Product Backlog item
Define the acceptance criteria for a Product Backlog item
Improve the acceptance criteria of a Product Backlog item
Identify dependencies within a Product Backlog
Estimate effort/size (e.g., story point, ideal hours, t-shirt sizing) of a Product Backlog item
Estimate the value (e.g., ROI, desirability, cost of delay) of a Product Backlog item
Define the next Product Goal
Evaluate the quality of a Product Goal (according to the team’s criteria or commonly accepted best practices)
Improve the Product Goal (according to the team’s criteria or commonly accepted best practices)
Define the Definition of Done criteria
Improve the Definition of Done criteria
Define the Sprint Goal of a target Sprint
Evaluate the quality of a Sprint Goal (according to the team’s criteria or commonly accepted best practices)
Improve the Sprint Goal (according to the team’s criteria or commonly accepted best practices)
Define the technical tasks for a Product Backlog item (i.e., decompose the Product Backlog item into smaller work items)
Estimate technical task effort/size
Design the event agenda (i.e., the sequence of activities and minutes per activity) and timeboxes for Sprint events (e.g., Sprint Review, Sprint Planning, Sprint Retrospective)
Capture/Identify action items and owners of Events (e.g., Sprint Review, Sprint Planning, Sprint Retrospective)
Identify recurring themes or impediments from event notes
Summarize what happened during an Event (e.g., Sprint Review, Sprint Planning, Sprint Retrospective) given notes, audio recordings, or video recordings
Summarize Sprint Review stakeholder feedback and follow-up actions
Define possible impediment mitigation options
Summarize impediment data (e.g., count, status, owner, resolution progress) into a concise report.
Evaluate the quality of a product roadmap
Improve the product roadmap
Summarize the product roadmap for stakeholders
Describe the product vision
Improve the product vision
Identify project/product risks
Identify possible mitigation strategies for risk items
Summarize key risks for stakeholders
Interpret burndown, burnup, or CFD charts
Define corrective actions (e.g., limit WIP, improve QA, breakdown items, start a spike) given metrics analysis (e.g., velocity, burndown)
Summarize Sprint or release progress
Define the team working agreement
Improve the team working agreements
